title: Jinja 3.1.6 Environments, Layouts, Autoescape, and Contracts · Guided Program Studio
summary: A civic-publishing pair incrementally builds strict autoescaped layout and component system from original fixed content fixtures while retaining earlier Python, Git, HTML, CSS, URL, accessibility, security, determinism, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of strict autoescaped layout and component system while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Create one configured Jinja environment with bounded loader roots, explicit autoescape, StrictUndefined, deterministic globals, and version evidence.
Pass a documented immutable view model instead of filesystem objects, services, secrets, or unrestricted application state.
Use base layouts, blocks, includes, and macros with explicit ownership, required regions, naming, and bounded inheritance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Jinja Environment
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop publishing team handles case 9289 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to configure strict autoescaped templates with explicit view-model and safe-markup boundaries; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create one configured Jinja environment with bounded loader roots, explicit autoescape, StrictUndefined, deterministic globals, and version evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-template-diagnostics-umk42y
# Competency: ssg-template-diagnostics.
# Case 3837: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_template_diagnostics_umk42y(templates: int = 6, rendered: int = 6, autoescaped: bool = True, strict_undefined: bool = True, unsafe_safe_filters: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_umk42y = "umk42y-y24kmu-3837"
    assert evidence_variant_umk42y
    evidence_axes_umk42y = (
        "readerumk42y",
        "source3837",
        "parsey24kmu",
        "routeumk42y3837",
        "artifact3837umk42y",
        "recoveryy24kmu3837",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_umk42y)) == 6
    if templates < 1 or rendered < 0:
        return False, rendered, "template-count"
    if rendered != templates:
        return False, rendered, "template-coverage"
    if not autoescaped or not strict_undefined:
        return False, rendered, "environment-policy"
    if unsafe_safe_filters > 0:
        return False, unsafe_safe_filters, "safe-boundary"
    assert rendered == templates
    return True, rendered, "strict-layout-system"
